topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus ADP 11.18 makes Lemon a likely 1.04 target if Love/Tate go 1-2 and Mendoza goes 1.03. Big-name USC pedigree with Amon-Ra St. Brown comp checks the 'name' box this GM chases.

topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: ADP 11.39 and the consensus QB1 of the class. With 3 QBs already rostered need is low, but a low-RSP-affinity GM chasing big names will absolutely take the top QB at 1.04 if he slides past 1.03.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: ADP 12.71 — 'considered one of the best WR prospects by much of the industry.' That industry-buzz framing is exactly what an ADP-chaser with low RSP affinity weights heavily. Realistic 1.04 fallback.

topTargets.name: Kenyon Sadiq
topTargets.reasoning: ADP 13.11 with Brock Bowers high-end comp and Combine fireworks — exactly the type of name-brand splash a consensus chaser loves at 2.15 to anchor a TE2 spot behind the existing two.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: ADP 13.57, first-round value per consensus. If 2.15 is on the clock and Concepcion has slipped (possible — boards have him as a fringe 1st), this GM grabs the recognizable name over an RSP darling.

capPosture: Comfortable at $36.2M used with $8.8M space and 7 contracts expiring — can absorb a 1.04 active-roster rookie deal without forcing taxi.

wildcard.name: Nicholas Singleton
wildcard.reasoning: ADP 25.83, Penn State name brand, top recruited back coming out of HS — the kind of recognizable college star a low-RSP-affinity GM reaches for at 2.15 when chasing names over Waldman's underrated picks.

summary: Music City Mafia comes into the draft with cap flexibility ($8.8M) and a need to backfill 7 expiring contracts, but as a consensus-ADP chaser with zero current RSP affinity, this GM is taking the biggest name still on the board at 1.04 — likely Lemon or Mendoza if they slide. At 2.15 they hunt brand recognition (Sadiq, Concepcion, or a Penn State back like Singleton) rather than mining Waldman's underrated tier. Expect a WR-first plan reinforcing an already WR-heavy roster, because that's where the consensus names live.
